delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: This course will help students build their spoken communication skills in English. It will introduce the conversation skills for asking questions, responding to questions, expressing one's opinion, and negotiating misunderstandings. In addition, the teacher will introduce examples of native-level language in order to develop advanced expressive skills. Students will have frequent opportunities to communicate with their classmates in English and practice the grammar, vocabulary, pronunciation and techniques they learn in class.
aims:
This is an advanced level speaking-focused English language course. This course will introduce a variety of practical skills for expressing oneself and conversing with others in English. In addition to skill building, the aim is that the course will develop speaking confidence and assist students in putting their overall knowledge of English to use in conversation.
As an advanced chukyu-level class, emphasis will be placed on independent speaking (not using a script) and conversing about opinions.
goals: Gain practice and confidence in speaking English
Guided exposure to native-level language, media, and culture

plan.text:
[Course overview]
Introduction and overview of the course, assessments and other requirements.
Meet your teacher and classmates
Student-selected Topic 1: Question Development and Discussion
Student-selected Topic 2: Question Development and Discussion
Student-selected Topic 3: Question Development and Discussion
Student-selected Topic 4: Question Development and Discussion
Student-selected Topic 5: Question Development and Discussion
Student-selected Topic 6: Question Development and Discussion

textbooks: Materials will be introduced/provided in class
prereq:
>Students should be willing to participate in class activities including spoken activities, group work, etc.
>Students should do their best to communicate in English during the "activity" time.
>Students must submit all assessments, including spoken presentations, essays, blog posts, etc., before the end of the last lesson (unless there is a written agreement with the teacher stating that the student can submit at a later date). If all assessment tasks are not completed, the student will not be able to pass the course.
>As this is a practice-based course, students must be absent no more than three times.
prep:
It is necessary to preview lesson content before class and review after class.
Students should complete any homework assigned in class.
teachers: ◎ デビット レスリー

extra.label: 区分1
extra.text: 平成20年度以降入学生／令和６年度以降入学生

extra.label: 区分2
extra.text: 共通教育）初年次科目／国際コミュニケーション科目基軸英語

extra.label: 履修における注意点
extra.text: １年次に必修。１外国語（英語）履修上限年間10単位。卒業にはⅠとⅡ両方が必要。

extra.label: 資格等
extra.text: 教免－省令で定める科目：外国語コミュニケーション

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
語学に関するリテラシー
表現力
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
Participation (40) Attitude and attendance, completing work, answering questions, and speaking in English during conversation activities.
Midterm Speaking Conversation Test (40)
Team Debates (20)

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
extra.text:
有
教員の実務経験との関連

extra.label: 授業形態
extra.text:
その他
Teacher introduces the lesson content. Students are expected to answer the teacher's questions and/or demonstrate that they know the vocabulary for each unit.
Students participate in pair or three-people conversation in English for approximately half of the lesson duration (45 mins). Pairs are chosen at random and students are expected to be willing to communicate with all other class members.
